name: Tetryzoline
description: Tetryzoline (also known as Tetrahydrozoline), a derivative of imidazoline, is found in over-the-counter eye drops and nasal sprays. Other derivatives include naphazoline, oxymetazoline, and xylometazoline. Johnson and Johnson manufactures tetryzoline eye drops under the brand Visine ®.
indication: For temporary relief of discomfort and redness of the eye due to minor eye irritations.
toxicity: Reports of prolonged cardiovascular effects after unintentional ingestion of tetrahydrozoline are noted in the literature.
mechanismOfAction: Tetryzoline is an alpha-adrenergic agonist. Its primary action is the constriction of conjunctival blood vessels, which serves to decrease eye redness caused by minor ocular irritants.

interactions.name: Iobenguane
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Iobenguane can be decreased when used in combination with Tetryzoline.

interactions.name: Tranylcypromine
interactions.description: Tranylcypromine may increase the hypertensive activities of Tetryzoline.
